title: Chemical Reactions and Equations
subject: science

Khan Academy is an American educational nonprofit organization founded in 2008 by Sal Khan. The organization provides free online learning resources for students and teachers worldwide.
The Khan Academy platform offers instructional videos, practice exercises, teaching tools, test preparation and assessment tests, across a range of subjects including mathematics, sciences, history, literature, economics and computer science. Lessons are typically delivered through short instructional videos paired with interactive practice activities.
Khan Academy is supported primarily through philanthropic donations, allowing its educational materials to remain freely available to users.
The origins of Khan Academy date to 2004, when Salman "Sal" Khan began tutoring a cousin in mathematics online using a service called Yahoo!…

sections.title: 2. Key Definitions
sections.body:
**Definitions extracted from crawled pages:**

- Khan Academy is an American educational nonprofit organization founded in 2008 by Sal Khan. The organization provides free online learning resources for students and teachers worldwide.
- The Khan Academy platform offers instructional videos, practice exercises, teaching tools, test preparation and assessment tests, across a range of subjects including mathematics, sciences, history, literature, economics and computer science. Lessons are typically delivered through short instructional videos paired with interactive practice activities.
- Khan Academy is supported primarily through philanthropic donations, allowing its educational materials to remain freely available to users.
- Khan Academy's lessons are primarily delivered through short instructional videos paired with interactive practice exercises and assessments.[10][11] Studies have found that use of Khan Academy resources is associated with improved student academic performance.[12]
- The videos display a recording of drawings on an electronic blackboard, which are similar to the style of a teacher giving a lecture. The narrator describes each drawing and how the drawings relate to the material being taught.[28][29] Furthermore, throughout the lessons, users can earn badges and energy points, which can be displayed on their profiles. Non-profit groups have distributed offline versions of the videos to rural areas in Asia, Latin America, and Africa.[30][31][32] Videos range from all subjects covered in school and for all grades from kindergarten up through high school. The Khan Academy website also hosts content from educational YouTube channels and organizations such as Crash Course and the Museum of Modern Art.[33] It also provides online courses for preparing for standardized tests, including the SAT, AP Chemistry, Praxis Core and MCAT[34] and released LSAT preparation lessons in 2018.[35] It also has a collaboration with independent chemists, which are mentioned in "Meet the chemistry professional". Khan Academy has also supported Code.org's Hour of Code, providing coding lessons on its website.[36]
- Since 2015, Khan Academy has been one of the official SAT preparation websites. According to reports, studying for the SAT for at least 20 hours at Khan Academy is associated with a 115-point average score increase.[48]
- "What is the history of Khan Academy?". Khan Academy Help Center. Retrieved August 24, 2021.
- "What is the history of Khan Academy?". khanacademy.org. Archived from the original on August 10, 2021. Retrieved August 10, 2021.

Khan Academy is an American educational nonprofit organization founded in 2008 by Sal Khan. The organization provides free online learning resources for students and teachers worldwide.
The Khan Academy platform offers instructional videos, practice exercises, teaching tools, test preparation and assessment tests, across a range of subjects including mathematics, sciences, history, literature, economics and computer science. Lessons are typically delivered through short instructional videos paired with interactive practice activities.
Khan Academy is supported primarily through philanthropic donations, allowing its educational materials to remain freely available to users.
The origins of Khan Academy date to 2004, when Salman "Sal" Khan began tutoring a cousin in mathematics online using a service called Yahoo! Doodle Images.[3][4] As additional relatives began using the lessons, Khan started recording instructional videos and publishing them on YouTube to make the material more widely accessible.[5] Early tutorials were recorded on Khan's personal computer using drawing applications such as SmoothDraw and ArtRage, often with the aid of a Wacom tablet.[6]
Public response, including coverage in USA Today, led Khan to incorporate Khan Academy as a nonprofit organization in 2008 and leave his job in finance to work on the project full time.[7] The organization was established with the goal of providing free learning resources to students worldwide.[8][1][9]
Khan Academy's lessons are primarily delivered through short instructional videos paired with interactive practice exercises and assessments.[10…

sections.title: 8. Practice Questions
sections.body:
_Class 10 CBSE practice for **Chemical Reactions and Equations**._

**Syllabus-based cbse practice set** _(auto-generated for review — Phase-II ingest)_

- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Balanced chemical equations** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Chemical Reactions and Equations.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Balanced chemical equations** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Combination reactions** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Chemical Reactions and Equations.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Combination reactions** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Decomposition reactions** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Chemical Reactions and Equations.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Decomposition reactions** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Displacement reactions** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Chemical Reactions and Equations.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Displacement reactions** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **SHORT (2 marks)** — Explain **Oxidation and reduction** in 2–3 sentences. Use one example from Chemical Reactions and Equations.
- **MCQ (1 mark)** — MCQ: Which statement about **Oxidation and reduction** is most accurate (Class 10 CBSE)? Review your notes, then justify your choice.
- **LONG (5 marks)** — Long answer: Describe the main ideas of **Chemical Reactions and Equations**. Cover at least three syllabus topics and one real-life application.
